ALSO BY NATASHIA DEÓN

Grace

' with regex '--USER__fontSize:(\d+)%;'
Font settings: Check of increasing and reducing the text size

No match found for text ' ch001.xhtml

Acknowledgment

The canvas displayed on the back cover was painted by Ms. Laura Scully, a talented artist; Ms. Scully and this work were both involved in my first experience of the presence of the Living God.

I would be remiss if I did not thank Naledi for her time, friendship, and insight during the writing process. Her spiritual fitness, kindness, and awareness comes from constant introspection and seeking God’s will in her life. Her experience and lifestyle offered me a constant sounding board to mold my experience in sobriety and with the Creator into words.

Thank you to Ms. Casey Ford of Saltwater Freelance Editing. Her ability to shape and reshape my thoughts and words without losing my voice or grasp of the true story leaves me at a loss for words. I am grateful for her faith, discernment, and willingness to pour her talent into this work. It has truly been a divine assignment and partnership with her. Her contributions were most essential.

Thank you as well to Ms. Larissa Lipani and Austin Macauley Publishers for their editing, design, and publication work. Their combined efforts ensure that the manuscript looks and reads as professionally as possible and is launched in an effort to reach those who need it most. May their careful consideration pave the way for “the one” to come to know peace and redemption.

Lastly, thanks and love to my parents and sister, Patrick, Gayle, and Katie, for their love and support throughout this redemptive process.

No match found for text ' Ruby Redfort: Look Into My Eyes

WHEN RUBY REDFORT WAS SEVEN YEARS OLD she won the Junior Code-Cracker Championships — solving the famous Eisenhauser conundrum in just seventeen days and forty-seven minutes. The following year she entered the Junior Code-Creator Contest and stunned the judges when they found her code impossible to break. In the end it was sent to Harvard University professors, who eventually managed to solve it two weeks later. She was immediately offered a place for the following semester but declined. She had no interest in becoming, as she put it, “some kind of geek freak.”
